---
title: "Project Charta"
subtitle: "Exploring the Aircraft Design Space:<br>An Interactive Visualization of 27’714 Aircraft Configurations"
author:
  - "Phineas Berdelis"
  - "Zina Aziz"
format: html
---

---

# Context and Scope

This project is situated in the domain of data science and aerospace engineering, focusing on the exploration of a large-scale aircraft design data set (“AircraftVerse”) which contains 27’714 simulated aircraft configurations.

Each configuration includes

- Design parameters: e.g. wingspan, rotor count, battery size, wing shape  
- Performance metrics: e.g. flight range, hover time, energy efficiency  

## Key Questions

- How do design parameters influence aircraft performance?  
- Which parameters have the strongest impact on the efficiency of an aircraft?  
- Are there non-obvious patterns or clusters in the design space?  

## Expected Benefits

- Users get a clear overview of all aircraft designs and compare different designs to understand variations  
- Users understand which combinations of design parameters lead to a better performance  
- Users identify meaningful patterns and clusters within the design space that are not visible at first.  

## Publication Platform

The visualization will be published as an interactive web application accessible to students and instructors.

---

# Project Objectives and Success Criteria

## Objectives

- Support comparison of different aircraft designs  
- Enable users to identify high-performing aircraft configurations using multiple criteria  
- Help user explore and understand how aircraft design parameters affect performance metric  

## Success Criteria

**Qualitative**

- Users can clearly identify at least three meaningful relationships between design and performance  
- The visualization is easy to use  

**Quantitative**

- The users will be completing tasks (for example finding the best design) in less than two minutes  
- The dashboard maintains a smooth performance with 27,714 data points  

## Out of Scope

- The project only uses simulated data. Physical hardware will not be constructed  
- No advanced predictive modelling (for example machine learning)  
- There will be no detailed engineering validation or evaluation  

---

# Stakeholder Analysis

- Data Science Students: Use the tool for exploration and analysis  
- Course Instructors/lecturers: Evaluate project quality  
- Aerospace Enthusiasts: Interested in design insights  

---

# User Analysis

## Persona 1: Data Science Students

- Domain expertise: familiar with data science and programming concepts, limited aerospace background  
- Technical environment: Laptop  

**User tasks**

- Explore the dataset and examine connections/correlations between design parameters and performance metrics  
- Identify patterns and clusters in the aircraft designs  
- Present the project to instructors at the end of the semester  

**Pains**

- A large, high-dimensional dataset can be overwhelming  
- Difficult so see relationships in plots  
- Flexibility and adaption are needed  

**Gains**

- Clear visual insights that show connections  
- Ability to filter, compare and explore designs  
- Discover patterns that support analysis and reporting  

---

## Persona 2: Course Instructors/Lecturers

- Domain expertise: Data Science and Visual Communication  
- Technical environment: Laptop  

**User tasks**

- Evaluate the quality of the students’ analysis and findings  
- Provide feedback  
- Conduct coaching sessions  

**Pains**

- No domain knowledge about aviation or aircraft technology  

**Gains**

- Ability to assess whether students’ visualizations highlight significant patterns and relationships  
- Confidence that the visualizations accurately and clearly communicate multidimensional data  

---

# Situation Assessment

## Resources

- Dataset: AircraftVerse  
- Tools: Python (Plotly Dash), Matplotlib  
- Team: 2 Students  

## Constraints

- Limited time (semester project)  
- Data complexity (high-dimensional)  

## Risks

- Complex visualizations  
- Technical challenges such as performance  

---

# Visualization Concept

**Product Form:**  
An interactive dashboard combined with a data story  

## Visual Encodings

- Scatter plots for relationships (e.g. wingspan vs. range)  
- Histograms for distributions  
- Heatmaps for correlation overview  

## Interactivity

- Filtering  
- Hover tooltips: detailed info per design  
- Zoom in on design  

---

# Project Plan

```{mermaid}
gantt
    title Project Timeline
    dateFormat  YYYY-MM-DD

    section Phase 1
    Data Understanding           :2026-03-19, 7d

    section Phase 2
    Concept Design               :2026-03-27, 10d

    section Phase 3
    Implementation               :2026-04-06, 35d

    section Phase 4
    Testing and Refinement       :2026-05-11, 14d

    section Phase 5
    Finalization & Presentation  :2026-05-25, 7d
```

## Phase 1: Data Understanding 

- Explore dataset  
- Define key variables  

## Phase 2: Concept Design

- Sketch visualizations  
- Define interactions  

## Phase 3: Implementation

- Build dashboard  
- Integrate user interactivity  

## Phase 4: Testing and Refinement

- User testing  
- Improve usability  

## Phase 5: Finalization and Presentation

- Final polish  
- Prepare presentation  

---

# GitHub Pages

Link zur Dokumentation: **TODO**
